prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f+, f−, f0) for aniline; headline figure reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui function value (f+)
Level of theory: Density-functional theory, tier “standard” = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF 2.5.0
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; single-point calculations for N, N+1, and N−1 states (no geometry optimisation)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for N state; +1 doublet (cation) and −1 doublet (anion) for N±1 states
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend PySCF 2.5.0)
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states. The integrity block reports status "ok" and trustworthy = true.
Warnings (verbatim from engine):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Live engine log: a live transcript was written during execution to the default chemkit log file (fukui_<timestamp>.out) in the working directory; it contains full SCF and charge-analysis details.

Result
The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C) with f⁺ = 2.2975867 (dimensionless). The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (N) with f⁻ = 0.20452359.

Headline value reported above ("value" field) is f⁺ for the most electrophilic atom (atom 3).
